_meta.description: Regulatory and policy sources affecting advocacy guidance, appeal strategies, and content freshness.

records.name: ACA — Affordable Care Act (Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act)
records.summary: Establishes essential health benefits, preventive care mandates, appeals rights (internal and external), coverage for pre-existing conditions, and marketplace insurance standards.
records.notes: Core regulatory anchor for most appeal strategies. ACA §2719 requires internal and external appeals. ACA §2713 covers preventive services without cost-sharing.

records.name: ERISA — Employee Retirement Income Security Act (Plan Appeals)
records.summary: Governs appeals rights for employer-sponsored health plans. Requires written denial notices with reason, 60-180 day internal appeal windows, and external review rights.
records.notes: Employer-sponsored plan appeals. 60-day window for urgent care appeals; 180 days for standard. Preempts state law for self-funded plans.

records.name: MHPAEA — Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act
records.summary: Requires that financial requirements and treatment limitations for mental health and substance use disorder benefits be no more restrictive than those for medical/surgical benefits.
records.notes: Primary angle for behavioral health non-coverage denials. Comparative analysis of medical vs. mental health treatment limitations required. 2023 Final Rule strengthened enforcement.

records.name: Medicare Secondary Payer (MSP) Rules
records.summary: Establishes when Medicare pays primary versus secondary to other insurance. Determines payer order for patients with employer coverage, workers comp, auto insurance, or other coverage.
records.notes: MSP questionnaire is the key document. Correct COB order must be established before appeal on PR-204 denials.

records.name: HIPAA — Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
records.summary: Establishes patient rights to access medical records, portability of coverage, and privacy protections. Specifically: right to request records within 30 days; right to restrict disclosure.
records.notes: Powers the HIPAA Records Request tool. 30-day response requirement; 60-day extension with notice. Providers may charge reasonable cost-based fee.
